title: GW231123: A Possible Primordial Black Hole Origin
summary: GW231123, the heaviest binary black hole merger detected by the LIGO-Virgo-KAGRA Collaboration to date, lies in the pair-instability mass gap and exhibits unusually high component spins. In this Letter, we show that both merging black holes may have a primordial origin with smaller initial masses. T…

title: Role of Reconstruction in the Inertness of Gold toward Oxygen
summary: The activation of ${\mathrm{O}}_{2}$ on Au surfaces is a fundamental step for heterogeneous catalysis and surface oxidation. title: Significant Phonon Chirality Activated by Crystalline Electric Field Excitations in ${\mathrm{KNdSe}}_{2}$
summary: Chiral phonons, lattice vibrations carrying finite angular momentum, are at the forefront of a fast-developing field for exploring and controlling quantum materials in captivating ways. title: Conformal Data for the O(3) Wilson-Fisher Conformal Field Theory from Fuzzy Sphere Realization of the Quantum Rotor Model
summary: We present a model for strongly interacting fermions with internal O(3) symmetry on the fuzzy sphere that (i) preserves the rotational symmetry of the fuzzy sphere and (ii) undergoes a quantum phase transition in the ($2+1$)-dimensional O(3) Wilson-Fisher universality class. title: Observation of Metal-Insulator and Spectral Phase Transitions in Aubry-André-Harper Models
summary: Non-Hermitian extensions of the Aubry-André-Harper (AAH) model reveal a rich variety of phase transitions arising from the interplay of quasiperiodicity and nonhermiticity. title: Symmetry Classification of Altermagnetism and Emergence of Type-IV Magnetism in Two Dimensions
summary: Two-dimensional (2D) magnetism, particularly 2D altermagnetism (AM), has attracted considerable interest due to its exceptional physical properties and broad application potential. title: Harnessing Plasmonic Heating for Switching in Antiferromagnets
summary: Nanoscale heating engineered through plasmonic excitation enables magnetic information processing and may provide a solution to the problem of heat waste, a major obstacle for green information technologies.

title: Robust Orbital-Selective Flat Bands in Layered Transition-Metal Oxyhalides at Room Temperature
summary: Angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy of layered transition-metal oxyhalides reveals intrinsic flat bands that remain stable at room temperature, providing a tunable platform for investigating strongly correlated electron states.

title: Hydroplasticity of Nanocrystal Films Induced by Mesopore Change
summary: A vapor training process allows for improved plasticity and better shaping of wood-derived films.

title: Dominant influence of vibrational entropy on the phase transition in CoNi and related high-entropy alloys
summary: Entropy is an effective means to stabilize the high-temperature phase of a material. title: Sound velocity, melting curve, and phase diagram of dense hot ammonia
summary: The sound velocity, melting curve, and phase diagram of ammonia have been investigated from 15 to 55 GPa and up to 3300 K using Brillouin spectroscopy of ${\mathrm{CO}}_{2}$ laser-heated ammonia samples in a diamond anvil cell. title: Fermi surface geometry and momentum-dependent electron-phonon coupling drive the charge density wave in quasi-one-dimensional ${\mathrm{ZrTe}}_{3}$
summary: ${\mathrm{ZrTe}}_{3}$ is a prototypical quasi-one-dimensional compound undergoing a charge density wave (CDW) transition via a very sharp Kohn anomaly in phonon momentum space. title: Magnon edge states of skyrmion crystal in nonuniform magnetic field

title: Effect of Rashba and Dresselhaus spin-orbit couplings on the stability of topological Fulde-Ferrell-Larkin-Ovchinnikov phases in one-dimensional Fermi gases

summary: There are multiple possible origins of transport anisotropies in metals and superconductors. summary: Metasurfaces are becoming important tools for the control of thermal radiation.
